mnemonics: I.B.I.S. stands for Inductive Bias In Systems. It reminds us that bias is the necessary Information or Background assumptions an Intelligent System uses to generalize from limited data.
keyPoints: Inductive bias is the set of assumptions a learning algorithm uses to predict outputs for inputs it has not encountered.
It is a necessary component for generalization; without it, a model cannot make decisions on new data.
Different algorithms have different inherent biases (e.g., a linear model assumes the decision boundary is a straight line).
commonMistakes: Confusing inductive bias with the ethical or statistical bias often discussed in data. Inductive bias is a necessary, functional part of an algorithm's design.
Assuming that a model with no inductive bias is better. In reality, a model needs assumptions to learn from a limited dataset and generalize effectively.
